no_overclaim_boundary: Loop-engineering registry is schema/declaration only; it declares the loop topology, fuses, and pause points.
Loop-engineering registry does not activate any runtime loop instrumentation in 0.65.0.
Loop-engineering registry does not modify the classic G1-G11 gate judgment behavior.
Loop-engineering registry does not auto-fire any back-edge; every back-edge requires explicit authorization.
Loop-engineering registry does not close RISK-037 (loop-engineering completeness); this slice (FX-188) declares the registry and a read-only loader only.
Loop-engineering registry does not claim 1.0.0 production-ready.

loop_gate_semantics.loop_role_note: primary role recorded; ADR §4 also lists loop-entry-gate (secondary, implied by setup-loop position)
primary role recorded; ADR §4 also lists loop-exit-gate (secondary, implied by setup-loop position)
primary role recorded; ADR §4 also lists loop-exit-gate (secondary, implied by inner-loop position)
primary role recorded; ADR §4 also lists loop-exit-gate (secondary, implied by inner-loop position)
primary role recorded; ADR §4 also lists loop-entry-gate (secondary, implied by middle-loop position)

PP-Plan-Approve.velocity_cost_justification: Plan Mode human approval is the highest-value checkpoint; it prevents expensive rework when the agent's plan diverges from user intent. The cost of a 30s-2min human review is justified by preventing hours of wrong-direction work.

PP-Reflect-SelfReview.velocity_cost_justification: Self-review before sending to Reviewer reduces review rounds (M7.4 §4.6 cost). One extra reflection pass costs seconds but saves full review-cycle latency.

PP-Fuse-Escalate.velocity_cost_justification: Fuse escalation requires human decision on whether to continue iterating or change direction. Mandatory because fuse-trip indicates the loop is not converging — continuing automatically would waste resources.

PP-Release-Gate.velocity_cost_justification: Release is an irreversible outward-facing action (per Coordinator boundary rules). Human/Reviewer confirmation is mandatory before any release.

PP-Back-Edge-Design-Replan.trigger: G9 fail + G5 concurrence + design-rooted cause
PP-Back-Edge-Design-Replan.velocity_cost_justification: A full Middle-loop restart (release back to architecture) is the most expensive back-edge. Explicit human authorization is required because the cost of unnecessary restart far exceeds the cost of the pause.

agent_intrinsic_loop.name: AgentIntrinsicLoop
agent_intrinsic_loop.governance_model: pause-points-inserted-at-defined-checkpoints; each pause must justify velocity cost
agent_intrinsic_loop.no_overclaim: schema-declaration-only; no runtime agent-loop instrumentation in 0.65.0

requires_all_of.concurrence: design-review concurs the root cause is design-level (architecture/selection), recorded in G5 findings, NOT merely a build/test/release defect

requires_all_of.human_approval: explicit human authorization to restart the Middle loop from design
